mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: Confidence Level is a C.L.U.E. (Captures the Likely Universe Estimate)

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: Confidence limits define a range (interval) within which a population parameter is estimated to lie.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: The confidence level (e.g., 95%) represents the long-run frequency that such intervals would contain the true parameter if the experiment were repeated many times.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: A wider interval yields higher confidence but less precision, while a narrower interval provides more precision but lower confidence.
